finalRosterBasis: players dressed in each team's final 5 regular-season games of 2025-26 — stars rested or injured in the final week show as 'returning', not new adds
gradeBands: A top 5 · B next 6 · C middle 10 · D next 8 · F bottom 3 (of 32, ranked by net value added: roster WAR change + pick capital in WAR7 — the same currency trades are graded in)
note: Offseason in progress — this ledger moves as signings land. Deltas are the projection model's read (talent-only, same limits as the projections page: WAR compresses elite defensemen and pure finishers). The grade ranks net VALUE added — roster WAR change plus draft-pick capital (WAR7, undiscounted, the same currency trades are graded in) — so a rebuild summer gets credit for its firsts; the points column is the next-season on-ice lens alone. A ranking, not a forecast. Players under an active offer sheet stay with the rights-holding club until it decides.

moves.why: Even by value, with Paul (depth, 31, the model values him at an early 2nd (~#41)) the biggest piece moving; the faint edge is TBL's. TBL sheds $2.3M of cap.

Even by value: Robertson (97th-percentile shot, 24) is worth about what the picks add up to — TOR cashes the asset, PIT bets the player beats the slots; the faint edge is PIT's. Robertson's value is a bet on his next contract, not a signed one. Priced at what these players would fetch, the deal tilts PIT's way by ~0.6 WAR — the verdict above prices realized value instead, where our lens has the stronger track record.
